text: Although iron is an attractive, nonprecious metal that is abundant in earth crust, emissive and photochemically active iron complexes are still hard to prepare as the charge-transfer states are deactivated very quickly, giving only few emissive iron complexes, none of which have been reported to show dual emission. We herein report a Fe(III) complex [Fe(ImP)₂][PF₆] where HImP is 1,1′-(1,3-phenylene)bis(3-methyl-1-imidazol-2-ylidene) which shows Janus-type dual emission behavior from ligand-to-metal charge transfer (LMCT) and metal-to-ligand charge transfer (MLCT) states. This unique photophysical behavior is achieved using a new class of ligands that contain four N-heterocyclic carbenes and two cyclometalating aryl rings. The presence of low lying π* orbitals of the cyclometalating units maintains the MLCT states rather than allowing them to be deactivated to LMCT states; a state which in this complex has a large lifetime of 4.6 ns and which is very reactive for reducing as well as for oxidizing the substrates. Our work provides a new model for iron-based photosensitizers and opens new perspectives in the field of photoredox catalysis using iron.
